_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**ACTION REQUIRED — Audit existing public-facing sustainability claims immediately.**
Any website page, case study, marketing deck, or RFP response in which the workspace uses the phrases "carbon neutral," "net zero," "climate neutral," "carbon positive," or any language implying the workspace, a related entity, or a specific shipment "does not add net carbon dioxide or greenhouse gases to the climate" or "has made significant reductions" to its GHG emissions is a potential trigger for Section 44475.2 disclosure obligations. The law is in force. The obligation is ongoing. Disclosures must be updated at least annually. Penalty exposure begins at the point of non-disclosure, not at a future enforcement date.
*Action:* Conduct a complete audit of all public-facing digital content. Flag any claim meeting the statutory language. Refer flagged claims to legal counsel before proceeding. **Legal Confirmation Required:** whether legacy claims published before January 1, 2024 trigger retroactive disclosure obligations.
*Who is affected:* Commercial leadership responsible for marketing copy, website content, and client proposals. Legal counsel.
*Why now:* The Act is in force. No grace period is provided in the text. Penalty accrual is per day per violation.

**ACTION REQUIRED — Inventory all voluntary carbon offset purchases.**
If the workspace has purchased voluntary carbon offsets — from any registry or program, for any calendar year — that were used to support any public sustainability claim, Section 44475.1 disclosure requirements are triggered for each project. Required disclosures include the seller name, registry, project ID, project name, project type (removal vs. avoided emission), site location, protocol, and third-party verification status.
*Action:* Pull all offset purchase records. For each purchase, confirm whether the offset was cited in any public claim. Compile registry and project-level data fields required by Section 44475.1.
*Who is affected:* Operations, sustainability function (if any), legal counsel.
*Why now:* The Act is in force. If offsets were purchased and claims were made after January 1, 2024, and the disclosures are not on the workspace's website, daily penalties are accruing.

**MONITORING — Assess applicability of Section 44475 if green shipping products are offered.**
If the workspace offers any client-facing product — e.g., "carbon-neutral shipment," "green freight lane," or similar — in which a fee premium is collected and the workspace or a partner entity issues or transfers an offset instrument to the client, **Legal Confirmation Required** as to whether this constitutes "marketing or selling voluntary carbon offsets within the state" under Section 44475. If yes, the technical project-level disclosure obligations in Section 44475(a)–(c) apply to the workspace as a seller.
*Who is affected:* Commercial leadership, product teams.
*Why now:* This is an emerging compliance gap in the freight industry; no published agency guidance has addressed it as of the date of this brief.

*Source: AB 1305, California Health and Safety Code §§ 44475–44475.3, California Legislature, October 7, 2023. https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/billTextClient.xhtml?bill_id=202320240AB1305*
